skill.name: startup-business-analyst-business-case
skill.description: Create an investor-ready startup business case with market analysis, product strategy, financial projections, risks, and funding rationale.
skill.summary: Guides teams through a structured startup business case for fundraising and strategic planning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and business terminology, not executable shell code or sensitive Windows files. The skill is still rated medium because its declared tools include Bash, WebSearch, WebFetch, and file editing capabilities that are broader than the documented business-case workflow requires.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Permissions Declared
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, WebSearch, WebFetch, and file editing tools. The instructions do not ask for shell execution, so publication should warn users that this community skill requests broad runtime capabilities.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata explicitly lists shell, network, and filesystem tools. I found no malicious command or exfiltration instruction, so the concern is over-permission rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Static Ruby or shell backtick findings point to Markdown code fences, inline filenames, and example tables. These locations do not contain executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown formatting for tables, examples, or inline file names. No shell invocation syntax or command execution directive appears in these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAM Market Acronym
low_findings.description: The scanner classified SAM as the Windows SAM database, but the file uses SAM in the standard TAM/SAM/SOM market-sizing context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line uses SAM with TAM and SOM or with market-sizing analysis. I found no reference to Windows credential stores or local security databases.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in prose about descriptions and business case content. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or password handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is business-planning prose, not security code. The file contains no implementation that could choose or invoke a cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding points to a go-to-market effectiveness bullet. It does not instruct port scanning, service discovery, or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is part of a business risk checklist. I found no network target, scan command, probing workflow, or reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Startup teams need clear business cases for investors and strategic decisions. This skill guides structured market, product, financial, team, risk, and funding sections.
actual_capabilities: Collects company, audience, market, and financial context before drafting.
Structures a complete business case with executive, market, product, financial, team, risk, and funding sections.
Prompts for TAM, SAM, SOM, traction, unit economics, and funding assumptions.
Suggests visual assets such as market funnels, positioning maps, financial charts, and roadmaps.
Offers a Markdown business case filename and sharing format guidance.
limitations: Does not verify market data unless the user provides sources or requests research.
Does not create a full financial model by itself.
Does not replace legal, tax, accounting, or investment advice.
Requires strong user inputs to avoid generic investor materials.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Seed Fundraising Case
use_cases.description: Turn startup inputs into a structured document for angel or seed investor review.

use_cases.title: Align Internal Strategy
use_cases.description: Create a shared business case covering market, roadmap, hiring, risks, and milestones.

use_cases.title: Assess Partnership Readiness
use_cases.description: Summarize the opportunity, differentiation, traction, and funding plan for strategic partners.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Business Case
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a first fundraising document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a startup business case for my company. Ask me for the missing company, market, product, traction, financial, team, and funding details first.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Pitch
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for teams with draft materials.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my pitch deck notes and turn them into a business case outline. Identify missing evidence, weak assumptions, and sections that need stronger investor support.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Financial Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for business model and projection work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my revenue model, pricing, CAC, LTV, hiring plan, and funding ask to draft the financial and use-of-proceeds sections of the business case.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Investor Review Pack
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a complete board or investor-ready package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete investor-ready business case with executive summary, market sizing, competitive analysis, go-to-market plan, financial projections, risks, milestones, and funding ask.

output_examples.input: A founder has a seed-stage B2B SaaS company and basic traction metrics.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a business case outline with executive summary, problem, market sizing, solution, traction, financial snapshot, risks, and funding ask.

output_examples.input: An operator has market sizing notes, a roadmap, and a hiring plan.
output_examples.output: The skill organizes the information into investor sections and highlights missing assumptions, source needs, and milestone gaps.

output_examples.input: A business development team needs a strategic partner narrative.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a concise case focused on market opportunity, differentiation, customer value, partnership rationale, and implementation risks.

best_practices: Provide specific customer, traction, pricing, and market data before asking for the final document.
Cite every market size, benchmark, competitor claim, and financial assumption.
Review projections with finance and legal advisors before sharing investor materials.
anti_patterns: Using the template without adapting it to the target investor or partner.
Presenting optimistic forecasts without assumptions, scenarios, or risk mitigation.
Skipping competitors, customer evidence, or the reason the timing is favorable.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured startup business case covering market, product, financials, team, risks, milestones, and funding needs.

faq.question: Can it research market size for me?
faq.answer: It can guide market-sizing structure, but users should provide sources or explicitly request current research.

faq.question: Is this suitable for investor fundraising?
faq.answer: Yes, it is designed for investor-ready business case drafts, but final materials need founder review and source validation.

faq.question: Does it build detailed spreadsheets?
faq.answer: No. It describes financial projections and assumptions, but it does not create a full spreadsheet model by itself.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Strong inputs include customer segments, market data, pricing, traction, costs, hiring plans, competitors, milestones, and funding goals.
